text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Thomas Bennett, DOB: 12-09-1984, Hospital No: U947315 NHS No. 6384201975  
22 Redmill Close, London, NW1 6DT 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy following previous mild head injury/occupational stress now reduced/insomnia improved/previous vitamin D insufficiency/smoker. 

Medication: Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily.  
Cholecalciferol 1000 units once daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg once daily. 

I reviewed Tom in clinic today. He attended alone and provided a detailed seizure diary from the last 6 months. Since our last appointment, he reports a marked improvement in overall wellbeing. He attributes this to the end of the peak season at the warehouse, with a notable reduction in workload and overtime. He is sleeping more regularly (6–7 hours/night vs 4–5 previously) and feels less fatigued. He has reintroduced short evening walks and has cut down caffeine. Weight is stable and mood is brighter by his account. Neurological examination today was non-focal with normal gait and coordination. 

Seizures: Tom has focal epilepsy. He describes his habitual focal seizures as brief episodes of left-hand paraesthesia with a rising epigastric sensation, sometimes with impaired awareness lasting under 1 minute, without secondary generalisation. Over the past three months he notes only rare brief auras without progression and no episodes of impaired awareness. Compared with earlier this year, his seizure pattern has settled and remains largely inactive at present. The last definite focal impaired-awareness event was in early June 2025; none since. He links this improvement to reduced overtime and better sleep at work since the peak season ended. No emergency department attendances. No injuries. Adherence is good and he uses a phone reminder system. 

Plan:  
- Continue lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily; no dose change given current stability.  
- Reinforce sleep hygiene and stress management strategies as these appear beneficial alongside medication.  
- Routine bloods (U&E, LFT, FBC, vitamin D) via practice if not checked within the last 6 months.  
- DVLA advice unchanged; he remains fit to drive under current guidance given absence of impaired-awareness events since June 2025—he understands to inform us immediately if this changes.  
- Review in 6 months, sooner if breakthrough focal impaired-awareness events recur. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Over the past three months he notes only rare brief auras without progression

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date: 02 Oct 2025. The patient has focal epilepsy with both focal aware auras and occasional impaired-awareness events historically. Over the past three months, he had only "rare brief auras" and no impaired-awareness events. Auras are seizures, so he is not seizure-free. There is no explicit numeric frequency given for the auras; "rare" is qualitative and cannot be converted to a per time unit rate. Therefore, per instruction 4, since seizures are present but the specific frequency cannot be determined, the result is unknown.
seizure_frequency_number: Over the past three months he notes only rare brief auras without progression

flaws.description: Gold (unknown) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
